data.title: Adani Group stocks climb after SEBI dismisses Hindenburg allegations
data.description:
(Reuters) -Adani Group stocks gained between 1% and 9.6% on Friday, a day after India's markets regulator dismissed short-seller Hindenburg Research's allegations of stock manipulation against billionaire Gautam Adani and his group.

Adani Power led gains among the nine entities with its 9.6% climb. Adani Enterprises, the flagship firm, rose 4.4%.

(Reporting by Anuran Sadhu and Kashish Tandon in Bengaluru; Editing by Harikrishnan Nair)

data.title: Adani group stocks climb after SEBI dismisses Hindenburg allegations
data.description:
Sept 19 (Reuters) - Adani group stocks gained between 1% and 9.6% on Friday, a day after India's markets regulator dismissed short-seller Hindenburg Research's allegations of stock manipulation against billionaire Gautam Adani and his group.

Adani Power led gains among the nine entities with its 9.6% climb. Adani Enterprises, the flagship firm, rose 4.4%.

(Reporting by Anuran Sadhu and Kashish Tandon in Bengaluru; Editing by Harikrishnan Nair)

data.title: China Properties Investment Mulls Fundraising to Strengthen Capital; Shares Surge 14%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China Properties Investment (HKG:0736) is considering a potential fundraising to strengthen its financial position and provide additional working capital, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Shares of the real estate investor jumped over 14% in Friday morning trade.

The company said the move would support ongoing operations and business development, citing recent gains in its share price as presenting an opportunity.

data.title: Iron ore set for weekly gain on improved steel demand
data.description:
SINGAPORE, September 19 (Reuters) - Iron ore futures advanced on Friday and are set to end the week higher, buoyed by strengthening steel demand and pre-holiday restocking in major consumer China.

The most-traded January iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) rose 0.56% to 805.5 yuan ($113.23) a metric ton, as of 0258 GMT. The contract is up 0.88% so far this week.

The benchmark September iron ore on the Singapore Exchange was 0.22% higher at $105.5 a ton, though it is down 0.19% for the week so far.

Steel demand is strengthening with the onset of the peak season, and restocking ahead of the Chinese National Day holiday is providing continued support to the ferrous metals sector. If downstream demand recovers more strongly than expected from late September to October, steel prices could see further gains, said broker Galaxy Futures.

According to data from consultancy Mysteel, China’s stockpiles of major carbon steel products declined 0.3% during September 12–18 from the previous week to 4.18 million tons.

Average daily hot metal output, an indicator of iron ore demand, reached 2.41 million tons in the week to September 19, up 171,900 tons from a year earlier, while blast furnace capacity utilisation rose by 6.29 percentage points year-on-year to 90.35%, according to data from Chinese financial information site Hexun Futures.

Top producer China's crude iron ore production was up 8.8% year-on-year in August at 81.63 million tons, while crude steel output was down for a third month on slow demand, data from the National Bureau of Statistics showed.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE eased, with coking coal and coke down 0.04% and 0.06%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange were mixed. Rebar gained 0.32%, and wire rod climbed 0.12%, while hot-rolled coil fell 0.21%, and stainless steel dipped 0.27%. ($1 = 7.1138 Chinese yuan) (Reporting by Lucas Liew; Editing by Eileen Soreng)

data.title: China Nonferrous Mining Hires Lawyers to Defend Against Court Petition in Zambia
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China Nonferrous Mining (HKG:1258) said it had hired lawyers in Zambia following reports of a court petition asking the company to take remedial steps after a tailings dam incident at the Sino-Metals Leach Zambia copper mining facility, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the mining firm gained over 2% in morning trade Friday.

The petition, filed by local residents, is seeking $80 billion for an escrow account that would be managed by the Zambian government for environmental restoration and compensation, as well as $200 million as an emergency relief and health and environmental assessment fund.

The miner said the claims in the petition and widely reported in media were "clearly unfounded," and that it would actively respond and protect its legitimate rights in the process.

The Sino-Metals Leach Zambia incident in February saw the collapse of the tailings dam at the company's copper mine, reportedly releasing 50 million liters of waste into the Kafue River ecosystem which supplies water to around 60% of Zambia's population.

China Nonferrous Mining blamed the collapse on theft and damage caused by heavy rainfall, and said it had taken immediate steps to rectify the leakage. It also highlighted statements my Zambian officials that stated the situation "had basically returned to normal."

China Nonferrous Mining said it had fulfilled its restoration and remediation obligations as required by government directives and compensated individual farmers harmed by the incident.

data.title: Copper rebounds as Chinese buyers return for holiday restocking
data.description:
SHANGHAI/BEIJING, Sept 19 (Reuters) - Copper futures rebounded on Friday from a one-week low in the prior session, supported by improving demand in top consumer China as lower prices encouraged buyers to return to the market to restock for the upcoming holiday break.

The most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange was up 0.19% at 79,950 yuan ($11,239.04) metric ton, as of 0406 GMT. The contract hit a more than one-week low on Thursday.

Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange added 0.37% to $9,976.5 ton.

Buying interest from downstream consumers have improved following a price fall as the week-long National Day holiday break looms, lending support to prices, analysts said.

"There are a lot of bargain hunt and restocking activities by downstream buyers, as the copper price is currently low and we are head towards China's National Day holiday," a Singapore-based analyst said on condition of anonymity as they are not authorised to speak to media.

Also, helping a rebound in prices of the red metal used in power and construction is "the expectation of more production cuts among smelters in September and October", analysts at broker Maike Futures said.

Chinese smelters may need to trim output as prices of byproduct sulfuric acid, revenue of which have offset loss caused by record low processing fees, have slipped, analysts at broker GF Futures said.

However, a stronger dollar, which makes commodities priced in the greenback more expensive for buyers using other currencies, curbed prices gains.

Additionally, investors await cues from a Friday call between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping.

For other SHFE metals, aluminium rose 0.17%, lead advanced 0.15%, nickel and zinc were little changed while tin dipped 0.57%.

Among other LME metals, aluminium climbed 0.43%, nickel added 0.35%, lead was flat, tin gained 0.68%, and zinc edged up 0.17%. ($1 = 7.1136 Chinese yuan) (Reporting by Dylan Duan, Amy Lv and Lewis Jackson; Editing by Rashmi Aich)

data.title: Sinochem to Transfer Headquarters to Beijing's New Economic Zone
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Sinochem Group plans to transfer its headquarters to a new economic development zone that Beijing plans to build for state-owned and technology companies, according to a Reuters report late Thursday, citing insiders.

The Chinese state-backed conglomerate will also move about 500 staff from its current Beijing headquarters to the Xiongan New Area, which is located in the Chinese province of Hebei. More staff will be relocated by early 2026, the report said.

The company did not immediately reply to Reuters' request for comment on the matter, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Vinci Subsidiary Wins EUR885 Million Rail Electrification Contract
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Vinci (DG.PA) said Thursday a consortium comprising its Cobra IS subsidiary and Elecnor was awarded an electrification contract for the Rail Baltica project.

Cobra's share of the contract is worth 885 million euros, according to a Thursday release. The work involves the electrification of 870 kilometers of rail in Estonia, Lithuania and Latvia.

Construction and concessions company Vinci said work will be carried out in two phases. The first phase worth 950 million euros is slated to commence Oct. 1, 2025, and end in 2030.

data.title: Capgemini Launches EUR4 Billion Bond Issue
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Capgemini (CAP.PA) said Thursday that it issued 4 billion euros of bonds in four tranches to fund its purchase of WNS, refinance debt, and for general corporate purposes.

The French IT and consulting company issued 1 billion euros of bonds due 2027 at a floating rate of three-month Euribor plus 0.30%, and 500 million euros of 2.50% bonds due 2028.

Meanwhile, the 2031 bonds and 2034 bonds, valued at 1.25 billion euros each, have a coupon of 3.125% and 3.50%, respectively.

data.title: Chinese Officials Call on Pig Breeders to Trim Output Amid Weak Demand
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Pig breeders such as Muyuan Foods (SHE:002714) and Wens Foodstuff Group (SHE:300498) were urged to cut their production volume to tackle slow demand, Reuters reported late Thursday, citing state-backed news agency Shanghai Securities.

Officials meet with the firms during a high-level meeting on Tuesday, where the companies were asked to trim the volume of breeding sows, as well as slaughter volumes. The weight of hogs are also asked to be capped at around 120 kilograms, the report said.

The call was made as hog prices dropped year on year to 13 yuan per kilogram from 18.8 yuan per kilogram, the media outlet said, citing consultancy MySteel.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Utilities Stocks Jump, Communication Services Struggles
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- The utilities sector advanced almost 2% at midday Friday, leading a broad-based rally.

On Wall Street, the Nasdaq composite index, the S&P 500, and the Dow Jones all reached fresh intraday trading records.

Frontier Energy (ASX:FHE) nominated to become a five-year fixed price facility after the Australian Energy Market Operator assigned peak certified reserve capacity (CRC) to the Waroona stage one renewable energy project in Western Australia. The project was assigned a peak CRC of about 88.1 megawatts

However, the communication services sector was the only one trading in the red, falling 0.5%.

SKY Network Television (ASX:SKT, NZE:SKT) will continue its partnership with Formula 1 in New Zealand through a three-year rights renewal, starting from the new season in March 2026. All F1 practice, qualifying, and race sessions will be broadcast live on Sky Sport and Sky Sport Now, along with highlights packages of all F1 races.

data.title: Gold flat as investors await more Fed cues after widely expected cut
data.description:
(Reuters) - Gold prices were flat on Friday as the Federal Reserve's 25-basis-point rate cut and the outlook on further easing in the months ahead failed to meet the investors' dovish expectations, while markets awaited more cues into U.S. policy path.

Spot gold was little changed at $3,646.23 per ounce as of 0311 GMT. Bullion had hit a record high of $3,707.40 on Wednesday.

U.S. gold futures for December delivery were also flat at $3,678.90.

"Sentiment is still bullish but has definitely cooled off a bit. Basically, the Fed didn't really deliver with the dovish guidance needed for gold to push higher," Capital.com analyst Kyle Rodda said.

"The forecast of only one cut in 2026 was above market pricing and has had the effect of pushing up yields and the dollar," Rodda added.

"We need something now to reverse that dynamic for gold to make another solid run above $3,700. Some weak U.S. data would probably do the trick."

The Fed resumed rate cuts on Wednesday and opened the door to further easing, but tempered its message with warnings of sticky inflation, sowing doubt over the pace of future easing.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell characterised the policy action as a risk-management cut in response to the weakening labour market and said the central bank was in a "meeting-by-meeting situation" regarding the rate outlook.

Traders are pricing in a 92% chance of another 25-basis-points cut at the Fed's October meeting, per the CME Group's FedWatch tool.

Lower rates reduce the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding bullion.

The number of Americans filing new applications for unemployment benefits fell last week, data showed, but the labour market softened as demand for and supply of workers have diminished.

Elsewhere, spot silver rose 0.7% to $42.11 per ounce, platinum gained 0.2% to $1,386.10. Palladium, up 0.6% at $1,157.49, was headed for weekly fall, losing 3.3% so far this week.

(Reporting by Brijesh Patel in Bengaluru; Editing by Sumana Nandy)

By Brijesh Patel

data.title: Moody's Affirms Centrica's Rating Citing Strong Financial Profile
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Moody's on Thursday kept the long-term issuer and senior unsecured ratings of Centrica (CNA.L) at Baa2, expecting the company's financial profile to continue to be strong.

The rating agency said the affirmation reflects the improvements in the operational performance of the British energy and services company's retail and optimization businesses. The outlook on the rating is stable.

data.title: Almost every second municipality without electric car charging points
data.description:
BERLIN (dpa-AFX) - Nearly 45 percent of municipalities in Germany do not have publicly accessible charging infrastructure for electric vehicles. This is according to a response from the Federal Ministry of Transport to a request from Dietmar Bartsch, a member of parliament for the Left Party. According to the response, 4,923 of the total 10,978 municipalities in Germany do not have any publicly accessible charging infrastructure.

"The fact that almost every second municipality in the country has no charging facilities for electric cars is an embarrassing failure of the transport revolution," Bartsch told the "Rheinische Post" (Friday). In order for people to switch to e-mobility, not only must the price and range of the vehicle be right, but the nationwide infrastructure must also be "top notch." "If up to three-quarters of municipalities in the federal states have no charging points, rural areas will once again be unacceptably disadvantaged," emphasized the Left Party politician.

Rhineland-Palatinate has the worst charging infrastructure

In larger municipalities with more than 5,000 inhabitants, the supply is significantly better than in smaller ones: there, almost every municipality (98 percent) is "equipped with at least one publicly accessible charging point," according to the Ministry of Transport.

Berlin, Hamburg, Bremen, Saarland, and North Rhine-Westphalia have comprehensive coverage (100 percent each). Rhineland-Palatinate is in the worst position: only about one in four municipalities (26.5 percent) there has public charging infrastructure.

The ministry's response also shows that the federal government has spent around €9.5 billion on promoting electric cars since the government requirement came into force at the beginning of July. Nevertheless, "the German automotive industry is doing worse than ever," said Bartsch. "One reason for this is the neglected infrastructure for e-mobility."/evy/DP/zb

data.title: Nvidia Commits GBP2 Billion Investment to Fuel UK's AI Startup Ecosystem
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Nvidia (NVD.F) said Thursday it plans to invest 2 billion pounds sterling in support of the UK's artificial intelligence startup ecosystem.

The artificial intelligence and accelerated computing company will collaborate with Accel, Air Street Capital, Balderton Capital, Hoxton Ventures, and Phoenix Court in providing new capital for British AI startups.

The investment will be domiciled in the US and activated in the UK. It will be used to develop innovative AI technologies, create new companies and jobs, and prepare the UK for competing in the global AI market.

data.title: Ningbo Shanshan Discloses Patrial Release of Share Freeze; Sharesd Down 4%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Ningbo Shanshan (SHA:600884) said a sequential freeze on 287 million shares held by its controlling shareholder, Shanshan Group, has been lifted.

This represents 12.8% of Shanshan Group's total share capital, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The release was initiated by court-appointed administrators overseeing the reorganization of the parent company and a subsidiary under China's Enterprise Bankruptcy Law.

Shares of the battery materials manufacturer were down 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Chicago soy, corn head for weekly losses on US harvest pressure
data.description:
BEIJING, Sept 19 (Reuters) - Chicago soybean and corn futures rose on Friday but were headed for weekly losses due to U.S. harvest pressure, while traders monitored progress in U.S.-China trade discussions.

As of 0301 GMT, the most active soybean contract on the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) was up 0.53% at $10.43 per bushel after two consecutive sessions of losses.

Traders are watching for a call later in the day between U.S. President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping for signs of progress toward resuming soybean trade.

Market participants were also awaiting more clarity on U.S. soybean and corn crop sizes. Some expect the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) to trim its national yield estimates for both crops in its mid-October report after recent dry weather.

"Soybeans are still stuck between a relatively supportive outlook and the complete absence of China as a buyer of U.S. beans," said Ole Houe, director of advisory services at IKON Commodities in Sydney.

"It is likely U.S. beans will see pressure as harvest ramps up."

In Brazil, crop agency Conab projected the country's 2025/26 soybean harvest at a record 177.67 million metric tons in 2026, up 3.6% from the previous year.

Corn gained 0.53% to $4.26 a bushel but is set for a weekly loss on seasonal harvest pressure. U.S. producers are harvesting across much of the corn belt this week, with only scattered rain delays.

"Corn is starting to see U.S. harvest pressure, so any consolidation is unlikely to last, and there is a real risk it will drag wheat and soybeans down with it as harvest progresses," said Houe.

Wheat added 0.29% to $5.25-3/4 a bushel and was set for a second consecutive weekly gain, supported by brisk export demand, though abundant global supplies continued to weigh on the market.

The International Grains Council said on Thursday it has raised its forecast for 2025/26 global wheat production by 8 million metric tons, to 819 million tons.

(Reporting by Ella Cao and Lewis Jackson; Editing by Eileen Soreng)

data.title: Hong Kong to Expand Yuan Usage With Bonds, Stock Trading Counter
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Hong Kong will boost the use of the yuan by issuing more renminbi bonds and settling some government expenditures in the currency, the South China Morning Post reported Thursday, citing Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury Christopher Hui.

The move will pave the way for mainland investors to trade locally listed stocks in yuan, the report said.

The comments followed Chief Executive John Lee's policy address on Wednesday, which called for strengthening the city's role as the world's largest offshore yuan hub.

Earlier this year, the HKMA and the People's Bank of China reportedly introduced measures to deepen yuan connectivity, including a 100 billion yuan trade financing facility, new offshore yuan repo programmes, and enhancements to southbound Bond Connect.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Chinese Ride-Hailing Firm Didi to Raise Investment in Brazil to 2 Billion Reais in 2026 Amid Expansion Plans
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China's Didi committed to double its investment to 2 billion Brazilian reais in Brazil's food delivery operations by June 2026 as it seeks to expand its market in Latin America, Nikkei Asia reported Friday, citing a news release from the Brazilian government.

The increase in investment was announced after the ride-hailing company's chairman, Will Cheng Wei, met with the country's president, Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva, the report said.

Didi, which faces stringent competition in China, also plans to expand its reach through the 99Food platform to 20 Brazilian cities by January 2026. The platform was set up in April this year, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Novo Nordisk Lays Off US Obesity, Diabetes Education Team
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Novo Nordisk (NOVO-B.CO) has laid off a team responsible for educating healthcare providers on obesity and diabetes in the US, Reuters reported Thursday, citing two sources close to the company and LinkedIn posts.

The team comprised several hundred staff and was part of Novo Nordisk's commercial division, according to the sources. The Danish drugmaker announced earlier in September that it was targeting 9,000 layoffs globally as part of a cost-cutting and efficiency drive.

Novo Nordisk did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: INDIA RUPEE-Rupee set to weaken on extended climb in dollar, US yields after Fed verdict
data.description:
MUMBAI (Reuters) -The Indian rupee is poised to open lower on Friday, dragged by lingering strength in the dollar and higher U.S. Treasury yields following the mixed Federal Reserve outcome, while the previous session's unfavourable tone will further weigh on sentiment.

The 1-month non-deliverable forward indicated the rupee will open in the 88.20-88.22 range versus the U.S. dollar, compared with 88.1275 in the previous session.

The rupee came under renewed pressure on Thursday after a brief respite in the prior sessions, failing to sustain its climb past the 88 mark. The local unit's inability to sustain its advance underscored the fragile sentiment around the currency amid dollar strength, traders said.

Price action on the rupee "remains whippy within a broad 88 handle", a currency trader at a bank said. Just when the setup had begun to look constructive for the rupee, momentum quickly turned against the currency.

Meanwhile, in a positive development for the rupee, Bloomberg Index Services is seeking views from investors on whether Indian government bonds should be included in its flagship global aggregate index, according to a notice sent to investors seen by Reuters.

The dollar index inched up in Asian hours to near 97.50, extending a 0.7% advance over the past two sessions. The dollar had initially slipped after the Fed's 25-basis-point rate cut on Wednesday and projections that signalled more reductions ahead.

However, it soon found support from U.S. Treasury yields, which like the dollar, regained after their post-Fed decline. Analysts said the move reflected Fed Chair Jerome Powell's press conference, which they said sounded less dovish than the projections suggested.

U.S. jobless claims data out on Thursday contributed to the selloff in Treasuries. The number of Americans filing new applications for unemployment benefits fell last week, reversing the prior week's jump.

(Reporting by Nimesh Vora; Editing by Rashmi Aich)

By Nimesh Vora

data.title: Hong Kong, UAE Sign MOUs to Boost Fund, Capital Market, and Start-up Links
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Hong Kong and the United Arab Emirates signed five memoranda of understanding to expand cooperation across finance, logistics, trade, and technology, according to a government press release on Thursday.

The deals, announced at the Investopia Summit in Hong Kong, include a mutual recognition framework for investment funds between the city's Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) and the UAE's Securities and Commodities Authority (SCA).

The framework will allow public funds authorized in one jurisdiction to be more easily distributed in the other.

Other agreements covered capital markets, start-up incubation, and innovation exchange.

Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing (HKG:0388) also signed a deal with the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange earlier in the day, aiming to deepen ties and improve cross-border market access.

Abu Dhabi's Hub71 entered into three separate MOUs with Hong Kong's Cyberport, MTR Lab, and the Hong Kong-Shenzhen Innovation and Technology Park, aimed at supporting start-ups in establishing operations across both regions.

data.title: Chinese Overproduction Forces Japanese Petrochemical Industry to Restructure
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Japan's petrochemical sector is struggling with persistent overcapacity, a problem exacerbated by intense competition from Chinese manufacturing, Nikkei Asia reported on Friday.

For the 37th consecutive month, the operating rate for ethylene plants remained below the critical profitability threshold of 90%, standing at just 81.9% in August, the publication said.

While Japan's ethylene production saw a 6% year-on-year increase to 427,000 tons that month, its total 2024 output of 4.98 million tons still fell significantly short of its 6.16 million-ton capacity, said the news daily.

This prolonged pressure from overproduction is compelling Japanese manufacturers to initiate significant restructuring efforts to ensure the industry's survival, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Veolia Urges Unite to 'Find a Solution' Amid Strike Action at Sheffield, UK, Site
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Veolia Environnement (VIE.PA) on Thursday called on Unite to "find a solution" with the majority trade union at its Sheffield, UK, site instead of continued strike action.

In an emailed statement to MT Newswires in response to Unite's announcement of an "indefinite strike action," Beth Whittaker, the chief human resources officer of Veolia Northern Europe, said the situation is "an inter-union dispute" and that the company will "continue to defend the Group, its partners and investors against Unite's campaign of defamation."

Whittaker added that the French waste and water management company and GMB, which have a long-standing recognition agreement for the facility, reached a multi-year pay deal earlier in the year.

Unite said 90% of its members voted to continue the strike that started in August 2024 to ask for the recognition of their union and for improved pay and conditions. The union represents 25% of the Sheffield workforce.

data.title: Indonesia includes tax amnesty bill in 2025 priority legislation, lawmaker says
data.description:
JAKARTA (Reuters) -The legislative body of Indonesia's parliament has included a tax amnesty bill as priority legislation this year, a lawmaker said on Friday, in another bid to generate state revenue from undeclared assets. 

Southeast Asia's biggest economy carried out two tax amnesty programmes, in 2016 and 2022, which collectively uncovered more than $340 billion worth of unreported assets. 

Taxpayers were offered lower-than-normal tax rates if they disclosed hidden assets, while authorities pledged to penalise those who did not participate.

The government at the time had promised not to hold further amnesties, but to improve tax compliance. 

The tax amnesty bill had been on the priority list from the beginning and was not suddenly included, lawmaker Martin Manurung told Reuters, adding it would still require a wider parliamentary vote at a later date, 

He provided no details of the proposed bill and referred Reuters to parliament's commission for finance affairs. 

It was not immediately clear what the bill would entail and Indonesia's finance ministry did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

(Reporting by Stefanno Sulaiman; Editing by Martin Petty)

data.title: RUBBER-Japan futures set for weekly loss amid supply pressure
data.description:
Sept 19 (Reuters) -

* Japanese rubber futures rallied on Friday on pre-holiday restocking in China, but are set for a weekly loss pressured by the peak tapping season in Southeast Asia and expectations of increased overseas supply.

* The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for February delivery was up 3.4 yen, or 1.1%, at 312.6 yen ($2.11) per kg as of 0204 GMT.

* Still, the contract has lost 1.17% so far this week.

* The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for January delivery dipped 70 yuan, or 0.45%, to 15,560 yuan ($2,186.35) per metric ton.

* The most active November butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE gained 30 yuan, or 0.26%, to 11,485 yuan per metric ton.

* Stockpiling ahead of China's National Day holiday provided support to rubber prices, said Chinese financial information site Hexun Futures.

* Still, rubber prices are expected to remain weak in the short term as Southeast Asia has entered its peak rubber tapping season, while expectations of a recovery in overseas supply are further pressuring prices, said Tonghuashun Information.

* The dollar firmed 0.6% against the yen at 147.88 ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy decision on Friday.

* A weaker currency makes yen-denominated assets more affordable to overseas buyers.

* South Korean automaker Hyundai Motor will seek to ramp up its U.S. output, producing more than 80% of the vehicles it sells in the U.S. by 2030.

* The third-largest automaker in the world by sales is targeting production capacity of 500,000 vehicles a year by 2028 at its Georgia factory.

* Automobile sales could influence the intensity of automobile manufacturing, which involves using rubber-made tyres.

* The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for October delivery last traded at 170.2 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.2%. ($1 = 148.0600 yen) ($1 = 7.1169 Chinese yuan) (Reporting by Lucas Liew; Editing by Eileen Soreng)

data.title: STO Express Logs 14% Rise in Express Service Revenue; Shares Surge 10%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- STO Express' (SHE:002468) logged a 14% year-over-year growth in express service revenue to 4.43 billion yuan in August, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

On a per-ticket basis, express service revenue grew 3% to 2.06 yuan.

Completed business volume increased 11% to 21.5 billion tickets.

Shares surged 10% during morning trading on Friday.

data.title: Indonesia's parliament body agrees to include patriot bond bill in 2026 priority list, lawmaker says
data.description:
Jakarta, Sept 19 (Reuters) - Indonesia's key parliament body agreed to include a patriot bond bill in its 2026 priority legislation list, a lawmaker said on Friday.

Lawmaker Bob Hasan told Reuters that the bill will be discussed further in parliament. He did not give further details.

(Reporting by Stefanno Sulaiman; Editing by David Stanway)

data.title: YTO Express' Product Revenue Rises in August; Shares Jump 4%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- YTO Express' (SHA:600233) express product revenue grew 9.8% year over year to 5.39 billion yuan in August, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

On a per-express-product-ticket basis, revenue fell 1.1% to 2.15 yuan.

Completed business volume jumped 11% to 25.1 billion tickets.

Shares jumped 4% during morning trading on Friday.

data.title: Yunda Holding's Express Service Revenue Increases 5% in August
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Yunda Holding's (SHE:002120) express service revenue grew 5.2% year over year to 4.12 billion yuan in August, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

On a per-ticket basis, express service revenue fell 3.5% to 1.92 yuan.

Completed business volume increased 8.7% to 21.5 billion.

Shares jumped 2% during morning trading on Friday.

data.title: Panasonic aims to develop groundbreaking EV battery in about two years
data.description:
(Reuters) - Panasonic aims to develop a new type of higher-capacity battery in about two years, potentially extending the driving range of electric vehicles in a groundbreaking advance for the Tesla supplier.

The Japanese company is working on eliminating the anode in batteries during the manufacturing stage to increase energy density through technology it says is likely to provide a "world-leading level" of capacity by the end of 2027.

If achieved, the improvement would lead to a 25% increase in battery capacity, Panasonic said. That would boost the driving range of Tesla's most affordable sport-utility vehicle, the Model Y, by almost 90 miles (about 145 km), at current battery pack size.

Alternatively, Panasonic could also use the technology to make lighter - and potentially cheaper - versions of batteries by keeping the current driving range and shrinking the battery pack size.

A company executive told reporters of the anode-free technology ahead of a presentation on Thursday by Shoichiro Watanabe, the technology chief at the group's battery arm, Panasonic Energy.  

This technology is also being pursued by multiple global battery producers. 

Panasonic's proposed design has no anode at the manufacturing stage. Instead, a lithium metal anode is formed in the battery after being charged for the first time. This would free up room for more active cathode materials -- nickel, cobalt and aluminium -- to boost capacity without changing the volume. 

Panasonic said it also aims to reduce the proportion of nickel, which is relatively more expensive. 

It was not clear whether the technology would help Tesla to lower prices and Panasonic declined to discuss specifics on manufacturing costs.

Reuters reported this month that Tesla's U.S. market share dropped to its lowest in almost eight years in August as buyers chose electric vehicles from a growing stable of rivals over the ageing line-up offered by Elon Musk's company.

(This story has been corrected to show information on increased battery capacity, not detail on Tesla range, is attributed to Panasonic in paragraph 3)

(Reporting by Norihiko Shirouzu; Editing by David Dolan and David Goodman)

By Norihiko Shirouzu

data.title: INDIA BONDS-India debt supply concern set to limit early decline in bond yields
data.description:
MUMBAI, Sept 19 (Reuters) - Indian government bond yields may trend lower in opening deals on Friday amid the potential for inclusion in another global bond index, but any fall would be limited as debt supply remains a major concern.

The yield on the 10-year benchmark note is expected to move in the 6.48%-6.52% range till the auction, said a trader at a private bank. It closed at 6.5139% on Thursday.

Bloomberg Index Services is seeking views from investors on whether Indian government bonds should be included in its flagship global aggregate index, according to a notice sent to investors seen by Reuters.

The index provider included India in its emerging market local currency bond index from January 2025.

"Prima facie, this is a positive development as it would open doors for more hefty inflows, but after the initial short covering, the market would be focusing on immediate concern which is debt auction, where cutoffs would be the key driver," the trader said.

New Delhi will sell the benchmark 2035 bond worth 300 billion rupees ($3.40 billion) later in the day. The auction comes at a time when market remains concerned about skewness in demand-supply dynamics.

Traders also await the borrowing calendar for the second half of the fiscal year, which is likely to be published before the end of September.

In a round of consultations with multiple market participants earlier this month, the central bank received suggestions to reduce the share of ultra-long bonds and cut weekly auction sizes.

Meanwhile, hawkish commentary from the U.S. Federal Reserve weighed on sentiment, even as the central bank reduced interest rates and indicated more cuts.

The 10-year U.S. yield inched further higher from the key 4% mark.

RATES

India's overnight index swaps are expected to extend their rise from the previous session.

India's overnight index swap rates moved higher on Thursday, with the longer end witnessing heavy paying.

The one-year OIS rate ended at 5.47%, while the two-year OIS rate closed at 5.44%.

The liquid five-year OIS rate rose 3 bps to settle at 5.7175%.

KEY INDICATORS:

** Benchmark Brent crude futures were down 0.1% at $67.40 per barrel, after easing 0.8% in previous session

** Ten-year U.S. Treasury yield was at 4.1102%; two-year yield at 3.5635% ** India to auction sovereign bonds worth 360 billion rupees ** RBI to set underwriting fees for sovereign bond auction worth 360 billion rupees ** RBI to conduct six-day variable rate repo auction for 1 trillion rupees ($1 = 88.2180 Indian rupees) (Reporting by Dharamraj Dhutia; Editing by Eileen Soreng)

data.title: Thai king approves PM Anutin's cabinet line-up, Royal Gazette shows
data.description:
BANGKOK (Reuters) - Thailand's King Maha Vajiralongkorn has endorsed Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul's new cabinet, according to a notice in the Royal Gazette.

The endorsement formally enables the cabinet to take an oath before the king ahead of a policy statement to parliament, the final step before ministers can assume their duties.

(This story has been refiled to add missing date in the dateline)

(Reporting by Chayut Setboonsarng, Editing by David Stanway)

data.title: Thai king approves PM Anutin's cabinet line-up, Royal Gazette shows
data.description:

Thailand's King Maha Vajiralongkorn has

endorsed Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul's new cabinet, according to a notice in the Royal Gazette.

The endorsement formally enables the cabinet to take an oath before the king ahead of a policy statement to parliament, the final step before ministers can assume their duties.

(Reporting by Chayut Setboonsarng, Editing by )

data.title: Shares of BHP Group Rise After JV Vicuna Launches Andes Drilling Bid
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Shares of BHP Group (ASX:BHP) inched up in recent Friday trade after its joint venture Vicuna shared in a late Thursday media release that it had launched a competitive bidding process for diamond drilling services as part of its 2026 exploration campaign in the Andes mountains.

The process includes site visits, bidder consultations, and strict safety and scheduling adherence, per the release.

The drilling will occur above 4,000 meters in the Andes mountains, targeting the Filo del Sol and Josemaria deposits in Argentina and Chile, the release said.

Invitations went to bidders in both countries and San Juan's mining chambers, with evaluation focusing on technical, safety, and social criteria, emphasizing local engagement.

data.title: India's Net Direct Tax Collections See 9.18% Growth in April-September
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- India's net direct tax collections rose by 9.18% to 10.8 trillion Indian rupees in the April 1 to Sept. 17 period against 9.915 trillion rupees in the corresponding period a year ago, according to data released by the Income Tax Department on Thursday.

Direct taxes, including corporate and personal tax, saw a 3% year-on-year growth to 12.4 trillion rupees on a gross basis in the said period against 12.0 trillion rupees a year earlier.

The data showed a 23.87% decline in the tax refunds issued from April 1 to Sept.17 to 1.605 trillion rupees from 2.109 trillion rupees a year ago.

data.title: Ningbo Shanshan Says Reorganization Plan Submission Deadline Extended; Shares Down 4%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Ningbo Shanshan (SHA:600884) obtained court approval to extend its controlling shareholder, Shanshan Group and its subsidiary Pengze Trading's submission for a draft reorganization plan to Dec. 20.

The extension was granted due to the complexity of assets and various creditor demands, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the battery materials manufacturer were down 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Japan's Nikkei extends record run, bond yields rise ahead of BOJ decision
data.description:
* Japanese chip shares lead gains after U.S. peers rally

* JGB yields climb, yen weak after jump in U.S. bond yields

* BOJ widely seen forgoing interest rate hike on Friday

TOKYO, Sept 19 (Reuters) - Japan's Nikkei share average extended its winning streak on Friday, climbing more than 1% to a record peak, ahead of an expected hold on policy from the Bank of Japan.

The rally in Japan mirrored gains on Wall Street, where stocks hit fresh highs following an interest rate cut by the U.S. Federal Reserve earlier this week, along with projections for further easing later this year.

Benchmark Japanese government bond yields rose to a two-week high and the yen languished close to 148 per dollar after U.S. Treasury yields climbed to a two-week peak.

U.S. labour market data overnight showed some rare recent strength, slightly tempering expectations for further Fed easing.

The Nikkei 225 Index jumped as much as 1.2% shortly after the open, reaching a record high of 45,852.75, and was last up 1.1% at 45,788.66. The broader Topix climbed 0.9% to an all-time high of 3,187.98.

The BOJ does not announce its policy decision at a fixed time, but it typically comes sometime after 0230 GMT. Governor Kazuo Ueda is scheduled to hold a news conference at 0630 GMT.

Overnight, all three of Wall Street's main indexes notched record-high closes, with the outlook for easier monetary policy buoying high-growth chipmakers and other high-tech shares. The closely watched Philadelphia SE semiconductor index surged 3.6% to an all-time peak.

Shares in the semiconductor sector led the Nikkei's rally, with chip-testing equipment maker Lasertec surging 13% to be the Nikkei's biggest percentage gainer.

Larger peer Advantest rose 2.2%, making it the top contributor to the index's gains in point terms, due to its significant weighting.

"Chip-sector stocks are again leading gains, but within that group, those seen as related to AI data centers are particularly strong, showing that investors are being more selective," said Fumika Shimizu, a strategist at Nomura Securities.

"It seems likely that the Nikkei will close at a record high again today."

The Nikkei closed above the 45,000 level on Thursday for the first time.

Despite the supportive backdrop of easier Fed policy and delayed BOJ tightening, there are signs Japan's stock market may be overheating. An often-cited gauge called the Relative Strength Index (RSI) was at 75 on Friday, well above the 70 mark that analysts often use as a threshold for overbuying.

Stocks are also supported by a softer yen, which boosts the value of overseas revenues and makes products more competitive for Japan's heavyweight exporters.

Toyota Motor advanced 1.3%.

The yen was steady at 148.03 per dollar early on Friday, following a two-day decline of more than 1%.

Japan's benchmark two-year sovereign bond yields nudged up 0.5 basis point (bp) to 0.885%, their highest since June 2008.

The 10-year yield rose 1.5 bps to 1.61%, a two-week high.

Although the BOJ is largely seen as certain to leave rates unchanged, a majority of economists polled by Reuters earlier this month expect a hike within the next few meetings. However, they are split on the timing, with bets centering on October and January.

Japanese consumer inflation figures on Friday showed price pressures cooled last month, although the 2.7% rise was still well above the BOJ's 2% target.

(Reporting by Rocky Swift and Kevin Buckland; Editing by Jacqueline Wong and Sherry Jacob-Phillips)

data.title: Samsung Electronics to Hire 60,000 New Employees by 2030
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Samsung Electronics (KRX:005930) plans to hire 12,000 new employees per year till 2030, bringing the total hire to 60,000, the electronics products maker said in a Thursday release.

At present, 19 affiliates of Samsung Group, including Samsung Electronics, Samsung C&T (KRX:028260), and Samsung Biologics (KRX:207940), are carrying out second-half open recruitment.

Shares of Samsung Electronics fell nearly 1% in recent trade.
